name: Hearty Roasted Vegetables
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and cut onions into large wedges.
2. Peel and cut rutabagas into thumb-size chunks.
3. Scrub and cut potatoes into thumb-size chunks.
4. Peel and cut carrots and parsnips into 1/2"-thick diagonal chunks.
5. In a large bowl, toss the cut vegetables with lemon pepper and salt (omit extra salt if using a lemon pepper blend with salt).
6. Spread the vegetables in a single layer on baking sheets lined with parchment paper.
7. Roast at 425°F for 35 to 50 minutes, or until golden brown and tender.
8. Serve hot.
Rutabagas are members of the turnip family and are often called yellow turnips. Parsnips are sometimes referred to as white carrots but are not true carrots. Both vegetables are commonly found in American markets during the fall season. This method can be used with most firm vegetables that typically take a few minutes to boil or steam: green beans work well, as do various other root vegetables.
